reasoning:
To resolve as YES, Texas must enact a law that fully prohibits the sale of THC within the state before July 1, 2025. While the Texas Senate passed Senate Bill 3 (SB 3), which would enact a full ban, on March 19, the Texas House took a markedly different approach. On May 1, the House State Affairs Committee unanimously advanced a significantly substituted version of SB 3 that avoids a total ban; instead, it creates a regulatory framework allowing retailers to sell low-dose THC consumables, such as drinks containing up to 10 mg of THC.

Given the unanimous bipartisan vote in the House committee, the full House is highly unlikely to restore the total ban. Even if the bill passes the House and heads to a conference committee to reconcile with the Senate's version, the strong preference of the House for regulation over prohibition makes it highly improbable that the final compromise will be a complete ban. Since the resolution criteria explicitly exclude partial bans, the chances of this resolving as YES by the end of the legislative session in late May 2025 are very low.
